articles.title: Fossil generation could rise with faster-than-expected growth in data center power demand
articles.summary: Electricity demand has been rising steadily since 2020 after more than a decade of little change. Between 2020 and 2025, U.S. electricity demand, as measured by net energy for load, grew about 1.7% annually compared with 0.1% annual growth between 2005 and 2019. articles.summary: When tensions around the Strait of Hormuz began rattling energy markets again, the reaction was entirely predictable. articles.title: Middle East War Revives Push for Domestic Energy
articles.summary: The Middle East war and the mother of all oil shocks—the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz—have exposed the global dependence on oil and gas as buyers scramble for cargoes and consumers once again bear the brunt of spiking energy prices. articles.title: Iran Warns Gulf Energy Assets Could Burn if Its Oil Facilities Are Targeted
articles.summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) warned Thursday that it could ignite a wider energy crisis across the Middle East if Iran’s own oil and gas infrastructure comes under attack. articles.title: DOE Unveils $1.9 Billion Electric Grid Upgrade Push
articles.summary: The U.S. Department of Energy is launching a $1.9 billion funding effort aimed at rapidly expanding the capacity of the nation’s power grid by upgrading existing transmission lines. articles.title: How Venezuela’s Oil Industry Killed Lake Maracaibo
articles.summary: Venezuela’s Lake Maracaibo, South America’s largest and oldest waterbody, is at risk. The lake is situated in the heart of the Maracaibo Basin, one of the continent’s richest oil-producing regions. articles.title: Trump Weighs Rare Jones Act Waiver as War Drives Fuel Prices Higher
articles.summary: The Trump administration is weighing a rare suspension of the century-old Jones Act, an extraordinary step that Washington has historically taken only during major national emergencies such as hurricanes or severe supply disruptions. articles.title: Tesla Wins License to Supply Electricity Across the UK

articles.title: Fujairah Oil Terminals Resume Operations After Drone Attack
articles.summary: Operations at the United Arab Emirates’ key oil storage and bunkering hub in Fujairah are gradually returning to normal after attacks earlier this week disrupted loading activities at several terminals. articles.title: Ukraine Hits Major Oil Export Pipeline Hub in South Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine on Thursday hit in a drone attack a key pipeline hub of Russia’s crude oil exports in the southern region of Krasnodar as Kyiv intensifies strikes on Russian energy and military infrastructure. articles.title: Iraq to Hold Oil Output Near 1.4 Million Bpd as War Strangles Exports
articles.summary: Iraq will maintain crude oil production at roughly 1.4 million barrels per day as the war disrupting the Persian Gulf continues to cripple the country’s export routes, Oil Minister Hayan Abdel-Ghani said Thursday, according to the state news agency. articles.title: TotalEnergies Freezes Fuel Prices in France Amid ‘Exceptional Volatility’
articles.summary: TotalEnergies is capping the price of gasoline and diesel at its stations in France to protect consumers from the “exceptional market volatility” since the beginning of the war in the Middle East, the French supermajor said on Thursday. articles.title: U.S. Natural Gas Prices Stay Calm Despite Global LNG Crisis
articles.summary: While Europe and Asia are reeling from the highest natural gas prices in three years following the closure of 20% of global LNG flows amid the Middle East war, the United States remains relatively insulated from the biggest price shock since the 2022-2023 energy crisis. articles.title: Annual U.S. crude oil exports decrease for first time since 2021
articles.summary: Annual U.S. crude oil exports in 2025 decreased 3% from 2024, the first annual decrease since 2021. articles.summary: The acquisition includes 30 high-quality net locations targeting Wolfcamp A, Wolfcamp B, and 3rd Bone Spring formations, along with Sundown's interest in a Battalion-operated well.

articles.summary: While recent comments from Pres. Trump eased immediate concerns about supply disruptions, analysts warn that geopolitical risks in the Middle East remain high, and oil price volatility is expected to continue.

articles.summary: The energy major seeks to align its legal home with long established Texas operations.

articles.title: U.S. exports of major transportation fuels in 2025 were about the same as in 2024
articles.summary: In 2025, exports of major petroleum-based transportation fuels, including distillate fuel oil, motor gasoline, and jet fuel, averaged 2.4 million barrels per day (b/d), about the same as the previous year. articles.title: BOEM: US OCS holds 65.8 billion bbl of technically recoverable reserves
articles.summary: The US Outer Continental Shelf harbors vast undiscovered hydrocarbon resources, with potential for sustained energy production for more than 100 years on current production trends.

articles.title: Oil tops $100 on intensifying Iran war
articles.summary: Oil prices have exceeded $100/bbl as the Iran war escalates further. A few weeks' closure of the Strait of Hormuz could push crude over $150/bbl, analysts say.

articles.title: Interior proposes rollback of Biden-era offshore financial assurance rule
articles.summary: The proposed rule change aims to lower bond requirements for offshore oil and gas producers, especially benefiting small businesses, but faces criticism from environmental groups who argue it shifts cleanup costs onto taxpayers.

articles.summary: This compilation highlights news, insights, and updates from Oil & Gas Journal and Offshore, both EndeavorB2B brands, concerning the war in Iran.

articles.title: Eni and Nigeria resolve license dispute, paving way for offshore development
articles.summary: The Nigerian government and Eni have divided a major offshore license into four parts, ending a prolonged dispute and enabling development of key deepwater resources, including the Zabazaba and Etan fields.

articles.title: Harbour Energy updates guidance to reflect LLOG, Waldorf acquisitions
articles.summary: Harbour Energy updates its 2026 guidance, incorporating recent acquisitions, with expected production of 475,000-500,000 boe/d and capital expenditure of up to $2.4 billion, reflecting growth in US deepwater and UK assets.

articles.title: Chevron signs HoA with Equatorial Guinea, GEPetrol increases Aseng gas project stake
articles.summary: Under terms of the agreement, GEPetrol increases its stake in the project to 32.55% from 5%.

articles.title: Argentina’s first long-term LNG export contract: SESA signs offtake deal with Germany’s SEFE
articles.summary: The deal moves Argentina into long-term LNG exports, with plans to produce up to 6 million tpy from offshore floating liquefaction units, primarily sourcing gas from Vaca Muerta in Neuquén basin.

articles.title: U.S. electricity generation in 2025 hit a record, again
articles.summary: U.S. electricity net generation reached a record in 2025 based on data from our Electricity Data Browser. Federal government, Alaska host lackluster Cook Inlet lease sales
articles.summary: No companies bid in a federal lease sale mandated by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, and only one bid for 20 acres was made as part of Alaska's annual lease sale.

articles.title: Petrobras authorized to import Vaca Muerta gas as Brazil pivots from Bolivian supply
articles.summary: The agreement aims to expand pipeline exports to 30 MMcmd by 2030, leveraging Argentina's growing unconventional gas production and addressing Brazil's need for cost-effective regional supplies amid Bolivia's declining output.

articles.summary: Services include engineering, project support, construction assistance, and digital systems management.

articles.title: Nuclear reactor restart in Japan will likely displace natural gas electricity generation
articles.summary: On February 9, 2026, Japan restarted Unit 6 of its largest nuclear power plant, the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa Nuclear Power Station in Niigata Prefecture, which was shut down following the 2011 Fukushima tsunami and nuclear accident. articles.title: Most natural gas pipelines built in 2025 connect the South Central United States to supply
articles.summary: Natural gas pipeline projects completed in the United States in 2025 increased capacity by approximately 6.3 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d), according to our recently updated Natural Gas Pipeline Projects Tracker. A substantial portion, 85%, or 5.3 Bcf/d, of this new capacity is dedicated to del

articles.title: Ten years after first Sabine Pass cargo, U.S. LNG exports are still on the rise
articles.summary: Ten years ago, on February 24, 2016, the first liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargo from the Sabine Pass Terminal was exported from the United States, marking the beginning of a new era in U.S. LNG exports. Today, the United States is the world's largest LNG exporter, ahead of both Australia and Qatar.

articles.title: Retirement delays of U.S. electric generating capacity may continue in 2026
articles.summary: U.S. power plant owners and operators plan to retire nearly 11 gigawatts (GW) of utility-scale electric generating capacity from the U.S. power grid this year, according to data reported to us in our latest Preliminary Monthly Electric Generator Inventory. articles.title: New U.S. electric generating capacity expected to reach a record high in 2026
articles.summary: U.S. power plant developers and operators plan to add 86 gigawatts (GW) of new utility-scale electric generating capacity to the U.S. power grid in 2026 in our latest Preliminary Monthly Electric Generator Inventory report, a record if realized. Solar power makes up 51% of the planned 2026 capacity

articles.title: Enhanced geothermal systems could expand geothermal power generation

articles.title: Maritime exports of petroleum products increased in January 2026
articles.summary: U.S. exports of refined petroleum products carried on clean product tankers have increased steadily over the last year, with exports totaling 6.3 million barrels per day (b/d) in January 2026, about 10% more than in January 2025 and near record highs, according to data from Vortexa. articles.title: U.S. natural gas production to reach record highs in 2026 and 2027
articles.summary: We forecast that U.S. natural gas marketed production will increase by 2% to average 120.8 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2026 and then further increase to a record-high 122.3 Bcf/d in 2027 in our latest Short-Term Energy Outlook (STEO). Around 69% of forecast production over the next two yea

articles.title: Many states slightly increased their taxes and fees on gasoline in the past year
articles.summary: State taxes and fees on motor gasoline as of January 1, 2026, ranged from a high of 70.9 cents per gallon (c/gal) in California to a low of 9.0 c/gal in Alaska. State gasoline taxes averaged 33.5 c/gal across states, a slight increase from their average last year.

articles.title: EIA forecasts lower oil prices in 2026 and 2027 due to persistent stock builds
articles.summary: We forecast that production of petroleum and other liquids will continue to exceed global demand, which results in Brent crude oil prices falling from an average of $69 per barrel (b) in 2025 to $58/b in 2026 and $53/b in 2027. articles.title: Electric vehicle sales fell as hybrid vehicle sales continued to rise in 2025
articles.summary: About 22% of light-duty vehicles sold in 2025 in the United States were hybrid, battery electric, or plug-in hybrid vehicles, up from 20% in 2024. articles.title: Record natural gas stock withdrawals during week ending January 30, 2026
articles.summary: Working natural gas stocks fell 360 billion cubic feet (Bcf) in the Lower 48 states for the week ending January 30, 2026, amid Winter Storm Fern-the largest weekly net withdrawal reported in the history of the Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report. articles.title: U.S. wholesale day-ahead electricity prices rose in 2025 with higher natural gas prices
articles.summary: Average wholesale day-ahead electricity prices at most major trading hubs in the Lower 48 states were higher in 2025 than in 2024, driven largely by higher natural gas prices to electric generators.
